messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following hotel guest registration privacy notice for GDPR compliance:

"We collect your name, passport number, nationality, and contact details as required by law for guest registration. We also use your email to send booking confirmations and, if you opt in, promotional offers. Guest records are kept for 3 years. You may contact privacy@grandhotel.com to exercise your data rights. Our DPO is available at dpo@grandhotel.com. We do not transfer your data outside the EEA. We use a third-party property management system (Oracle Hospitality) under a signed data processing agreement."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PASS] Legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c)) for passport and registration data. Consent (Art. 6(1)(a)) for promotional emails via opt-in. Contract (Art. 6(1)(b)) implied for booking confirmations. Multiple bases correctly applied.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] Passport and nationality data can imply ethnic origin. No acknowledgement of this risk is stated, though the processing basis is legal obligation.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Three purposes clearly separated: legal registration, transactional email, and optional marketing.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Data collected is standard and legally required for hotel registration.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] 3-year retention clearly stated.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PARTIAL] A contact email is provided for exercising rights, but the specific rights available are not individually listed.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PASS] Oracle Hospitality named as processor with a signed DPA.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PASS] No EEA transfers confirmed.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [PASS] DPO contact disclosed at dpo@grandhotel.com.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [FAIL] Not addressed.

VERDICT: Partially Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 15-21 (individual rights not enumerated), Art. 33 (breach notification absent)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- List all data subject rights individually with instructions for exercising each
- Add breach notification commitment (72-hour supervisory authority notification)
- Acknowledge nationality/passport data may imply ethnic origin and confirm legal obligation basis covers this
